name: Nifty 50
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **NIFTY 24,360.45 (+0.12%) / SENSEX 77,949.38 (-0.01%) — INDIAN EQUITIES STILL UNDERPERFORM PEACE-DIVIDEND REGIME (S&P +1.5% RECORD; KOSPI +1.4% RECORD; NIKKEI +5.7% GOLDEN WEEK CATCH-UP).** Gift Nifty +0.67% pre-open suggests continued firmness. India VIX -6.88% on the week. **FII flow stress visible:** sustained outflows ₹3,621.6 cr on May 5; week net ₹13,771 cr selling vs DII net buying ₹11,585 cr — **DII absorption ratio 84% (LOWER than prior weeks' ~90%, suggesting domestic absorption capacity is being TESTED.** The gap between Indian-equity performance and global risk-on widens slightly — S&P, KOSPI, MSCI EM all rallied; Nifty's +0.12% remains within the underperformance band but is positive on the day. **The Araghchi-India visit + MOU framework + Brent collapse compound:** if MOU signs within 7-10 days, the deployment-relevant question is whether the FII rotation reverses — with crude at $101, USD/INR at ₹94.74, CAD pressure easing, the structural argument for India re-rates favorably; the question is timing of FPI return. Bank Nifty 55,969.15 (-0.0% modest). 200-DMA at ~24,500 still capping; structural breakout requires (a) MOU signature, (b) FII rotation reversal, (c) ₹93-handle USD/INR. AI flows continue to bypass India IT services (Apple-Samsung-Intel halo to Korea/Taiwan/US). FII CY26 outflow already exceeds CY25 full-year ₹1.66T. FPI ownership ~16% (2-decade low, below domestic).
